activation_results.reason: Only the leading verb changes; the request still matches the canonical use case.

criterion_results.text: No American spellings present in the narrative (color, optimize, behavior, organize, center, defense, license as a verb, analyze) — Australian or neutral forms both pass
criterion_results.evidence: Narrative contains no banned US spellings; wording is neutral (e.g., 'cross-platform', 'cleaned up').

criterion_results.text: Output is machine-readable where the description implies (valid JSON/SQL/YAML)
criterion_results.evidence: Description implies a markdown report, not JSON/SQL/YAML; artefact is well-formed markdown with valid tables.

criterion_results.text: Report includes the canonical sections: 'Totals', 'Claude process tree'
criterion_results.evidence: Both '## Totals' and '## Claude process tree' headings present in the artefact.

judge_result.notes: The example artefact is a clean, well-structured markdown report containing both required canonical sections, uses neutral/Australian-compatible spelling, and is machine-readable in the markdown form the description implies. All three criteria are satisfied.
